name: GTM Customer Follow-Up
description: One approved message delivered through an exact communication target, with full provider-safe evidence and separately approved CRM follow-up records.
audience: Sales, success, and support teams sending one approved HubSpot transactional follow-up
problem: Customer follow-up is risky when recipient eligibility, transactional intent, target policy, template data, send idempotency, and CRM activity logging are implicit.
outcome: One approved message delivered through an exact communication target, with full provider-safe evidence and separately approved CRM follow-up records.
whyAi: AI can prepare the bounded message and activity context while StackOS resolves the named target, enforces policy, executes the provider action, and records audit.
operatorPath: Provide the follow-up goal, approved transactional template packet, and stable delivery key.
Review recipient eligibility, legal basis, target, content data, and desired CRM activity records.
Approve the one-to-one send and any CRM activity writes separately.
agentPath: Validate that the message is transactional, one-to-one, and supported by the exact named target.
Send through communication.send with the workflow delivery key.
Optionally record approved note, task, call, or meeting activity and reconcile all evidence.
recovery: If the target, entitlement, consent, relationship, legal basis, or template ref is unresolved, stop before sending.
If send state is incomplete or uncertain, inspect the existing communication and action evidence before any retry.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with the reviewed message packet when send approval is absent.
Stop after delivery when CRM activity recording is not authorized.
prerequisites: A HubSpot OAuth connection with CRM Core and Transactional Communications enabled
The Transactional Email add-on confirmed for the connected portal
A communication profile and exact target named hubspot-customer-follow-up
A provider-verified transactional template ref and explicit recipient/legal policy
proof: communication.send result with message, event, action-call, request, and idempotency evidence
Approved CRM activity receipts or an explicit no-record decision
whenToUse: One known customer or contact needs an approved transactional follow-up and optional CRM activity record.

stages.title: Bound Customer Follow-Up
stages.summary: Read the exact goal, target, message packet, activity plan, recent evidence, and customer context.

stages.title: Prepare Follow-Up Packet
stages.summary: Produce the exact provider-neutral communication content and separately reviewable CRM activity payloads.

stages.title: Send Approved Follow-Up
stages.summary: Deliver once through communication.send to the exact named target.

stages.title: Record Approved CRM Activity
stages.summary: Optionally create approved HubSpot note, task, call, or meeting records using safe associations.

stages.title: Reconcile Follow-Up Evidence
stages.summary: Store durable touchpoint and task state and report the exact observed delivery and CRM outcomes.
